title: centranthus calcitrapae subsp. clausonis
intro: Centranthus calcitrapae subsp. clausonis, also known as the white-stemmed centranthus, is a perennial plant that belongs to the valerian family and is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Centranthus calcitrapae subsp. clausonis include its ability to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the leaves and flowers, and then infusing them in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
